quoteFirst: Theatrical performers, and operatic _artistes_ above all, dine at a comparatively early hour.
quoteLast: , Adéonne made Eusebe kneel down before her, while she arranged his hair with the care of a mother who dresses the hair of her son.
title: Human Follies (La Bêtise Humaine.)
author: Noriac, Jules, 1827-1882; Marlow, George [Translator]

quoteFirst: We knew with all Paris of this affair, but were ignorant of the place of the marriage and the part M. de Lorges had had in it, when the third day after the adventure I was startled out of my sleep
quoteLast: in the morning, and saw my curtains and my windows open at the same time, and Madame de Saint-Simon and her brother (M. de Lorges) before me.  They related to me all that had occurred, and then went away to consult with a skilful person what course to adopt, leaving me to dress.
title: Memoirs of Louis XIV and His Court and of the Regency — Volume 06
author: Saint-Simon, Louis de Rouvroy, duc de, 1675-1755

quoteFirst: The artillery trains arrive at Wavre
title:
The Campaign of Waterloo: A Military History
Third Edition
author: Ropes, John Codman, 1836-1899

quoteFirst: They told us at the lumber camp that there would be three or four teams go down to the railroad station at Driftwood the next morning with spars which they were hauling to the river to raft. We got a man with a yoke of oxen and a bobsled to go with us and get the bear and the next morning about
quoteLast: we got the bear strapped onto one of the spars and started down the stream to the railroad and we shipped it, without removing the skin, to New York, where we got either $26 or $28 for it.
title:
Fifty Years a Hunter and Trapper
Autobiography, experiences and observations of Eldred Nathaniel Woodcock during his fifty years of hunting and trapping.
author: Woodcock, E. N. (Eldred Nathaniel), 1846-1917; Harding, A. R. (Arthur Robert), 1871-1930 [Editor]

quoteLast: IN THE AFTERNOON—THE LOST ONE—THE DESPAIR OF NEB—SEARCH TO THE NORTHWARD—THE ISLAND—A NIGHT OF ANGUISH—THE FOG OF THE MORNING—NEB SWIMMING—SIGHT OF THE LAND—FORDING THE CHANNEL.
title: The Mysterious Island
author: Verne, Jules, 1828-1905; White, Stephen W. (Stephen William), 1840-1914 [Translator]

quoteLast: in the morning the signal was given. The soldiers of the fourth regiment of artillery were roused by the beating of the _assemblée_. They rushed, half-dressed, on to their parade-ground. Louis Napoleon, whose fate it was never to be ready, was not prompt even on this occasion; he was finishing two letters to his mother.
title: France in the Nineteenth Century
author: Latimer, Elizabeth Wormeley, 1822-1904

quoteLast: a reporter of the _Star_ newspaper came to see Oscar, a Mr. Marlowe, who is now editor of _The Daily Mail_, but again Oscar refused to see him and sent Ross. Mr.
title: Oscar Wilde, His Life and Confessions. Volume 1 (of 2)
author: Harris, Frank, 1856-1931

quoteLast: came the stranger, and shortly afterwards the dinner. He had divested himself of his brown paper parcel, but had made no alteration in his attire, and was, if possible, more loquacious than ever.
title: The Posthumous Papers of the Pickwick Club, v. 1 (of 2)
author: Dickens, Charles, 1812-1870; Aldin, Cecil, 1870-1935 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: Several of these "dry rivers," which we passed in the course of the day, have broad valleys, which, if we may judge from a comparison with that we are descending, must have an extent of more than one hundred miles, draining a wide expanse of country of the surplus water in the rainy season, but remaining dry during great part of the year.
quoteLast: we encamped, having travelled twenty-six miles due east. The hunters were immediately sent out, but returned without game, having seen nothing.
title: James's Account of S. H. Long's Expedition, 1819-1820, part 3
author: James, Edwin, 1797-1861; Long, Stephen H. (Stephen Harriman), 1784-1864; Say, Thomas, 1787-1834; Thwaites, Reuben Gold, 1853-1913 [Editor]

quoteLast: . Perhaps I may have something to tell you."
title: The Monomaniac (La bête humaine)
author: Zola, Émile, 1840-1902; Vizetelly, Edward [Translator]
